campus: Genoa (DICCA - Department of Civil, Chemical and Environmental Engineering)
admission_type: open access (free access, but admission requires meeting curricular requirements and, where applicable, verification of personal preparation)

academic_requirements.summary: Open-access (non-competitive) master's programme taught in English. Admission requires a recognised bachelor's degree (at least 180 CFU/ECTS), satisfaction of curricular CFU requirements, English B2, and verification of personal preparation when the bachelor's grade is below the published threshold.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's (first-cycle) degree of at least 180 CFU/ECTS or an equivalent recognised qualification.
academic_requirements.curricular_requirements: Direct curricular access for holders of a Laurea (first-cycle degree) in Class L-7 Civil and Environmental Engineering or Class L-9 Industrial Engineering. Otherwise, the candidate must hold at least 36 ECTS in the Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry areas and at least 12 ECTS in specific engineering SSDs (ICAR/01, ICAR/02, ICAR/03, ICAR/07, ICAR/08, ICAR/09, ING-IND/24, ING-IND/25).
academic_requirements.minimum_grade: The personal-preparation interview is waived for holders of a bachelor's degree with a final grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum (or corresponding to ECTS classification 'A' or 'B'); for Italian degrees the published waiver threshold is a final mark above 92/110. For non-EU applicants the degree grade must correspond to at least 80% of the maximum possible grade (per the UniGeApply English master's selection page).
academic_requirements.personal_preparation_assessment: Candidates below the grade threshold undergo an interview (for Italian degree holders) or a documentation review plus possible interview/test (for international applicants) covering the fundamentals of engineering and the subjects characterising the environmental sector.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad with a foreign qualification apply via UniGeApply for pre-evaluation; eligibility of the foreign qualification is recognised by the Degree Programme Board based on the academic qualification, examinations carried out, and the CV. Minimum degree grade of 80% of the maximum applies.

language_requirements.required_level: English B2 level or higher (C1, C2).
accepted_proofs: An English-language certificate at level B2 or higher among those accepted by the University (listed on the CLAT page, https://clat.unige.it/CertificazioniRiconosciute).
A bachelor's degree taught in English, certified by an official document.
Passing the online B2 assessment test organised by the University's Polytechnic School (CLAT language skills sector).
exemption_rules: Candidates holding a B2-or-higher (C1/C2) English certificate, or whose degree was taught in English, do not need to take the University's English test.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: B2 English is verified at the admission/assessment stage; non-EU applicants have it verified during the UniGeApply pre-evaluation. No Italian-language requirement applies because the programme is taught in English.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of personal preparation (assessment of adequacy of personal readiness) - NOT a TOLC or competitive entrance exam. Interview / documentation review.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Candidates whose bachelor's final grade is below 9/10 of the maximum (or below ECTS 'A'/'B'; for Italian degrees below 92/110). International applicants are assessed via documentation review and a possible interview/test.
entry_exam_or_test.format: Interview for Italian degree holders; documentation review and possible interview/test for international applicants, covering the fundamentals of engineering and the subjects characterising the environmental sector. A separate online English B2 test is administered to candidates without an accepted certificate.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] The official requisites page currently publishes assessment-session dates for an earlier academic year (interviews on 6 and 26 September, 14 November, 16 January and 26 February). A.Y. 2026/2027-specific interview dates are not yet posted on the page; equivalent autumn-2026 / early-2027 sessions are expected.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Waived for bachelor's degrees with a final grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum (ECTS 'A'/'B'); Italian degrees above 92/110. Non-EU minimum degree grade is 80% of the maximum.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
First-year master's pre-registration: from 9 April to 30 November 2026 (and until 10 March 2027 with late payment).
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation of enrolment from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment); enrolment in years after the first runs 15 July to 5 October 2026 (arrears 6 October to 5 November 2026).
application_deadline_eu.notes: Open-access (non-ranked) programme; EU/resident applicants below the grade threshold must pass the personal-preparation verification before enrolling. First tuition installment approx. EUR 136 (EUR 16 stamp duty + EUR 120 regional tax) at enrolment; second installment by 10 December 2026; third by 23 June 2027.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
No programme-specific early closure is published for Environmental Engineering; it uses the standard fall-session close of 20 March 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After receiving eligibility via UniGeApply, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment (from 4 May 2026) and then apply for the study visa at the Italian Embassy.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published for this programme (uses the standard fall-session close of 20 March 2026).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Environmental Engineering appears on the UniGeApply English master's pre-evaluation selection page with a minimum degree grade of 80% of the maximum. Non-EU visa applicants use the fall session (max 2 courses; EUR 30 fixed fee). The A.Y. 2026/27 fall window has now closed.

required_documents.notes: Educational background, examinations, additional courses, work or internship activities; used by the Degree Programme Board to assess the foreign qualification.
At least 180 CFU/ECTS; degree grade at least 80% of maximum for non-EU pre-evaluation.
List of exams with credits and grades; used to verify the curricular CFU requirements (36 ECTS Maths/Physics/Chemistry + 12 ECTS engineering SSDs, unless directly admitted via L-7/L-9).

required_documents.document_name: English Language Proficiency Certificate (B2 or higher) or English-medium-of-instruction declaration
required_documents.notes: B2 certificate among those accepted by the University, or a degree taught in English officially documented; otherwise candidates sit the University's online B2 English test.
Uploaded on UniGeApply in jpg format; other documents in PDF (max 2 MB).

required_documents.document_name: Legalisation or CIMEA Statement of Verification, plus Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU visa applicants with a foreign qualification to complete enrolment. Knowledge of Italian is not required because the course is taught in English.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment (from 4 May 2026) after UniGeApply eligibility, then apply for the study visa.

source_quotes.quote: Environmental Engineering, Course Code 11925, Degree Class LM-35 R (Environmental Engineering), Teaching Language English, 120 CFU, 2 years, Genova (DICCA - Department of Civil, Chemical and Environmental Engineering).

Laurea (First cycle degree) related to the following Classes L-7 Civil and Environmental Engineering or L-9 Industrial Engineering ... [otherwise] Minimum 36 ECTS in Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry areas; Minimum 12 ECTS in specific engineering disciplines (ICAR/01, ICAR/02, ICAR/03, ICAR/07, ICAR/08, ICAR/09, ING-IND/24, ING-IND/25); English language, B2 level or equivalent.

You do not need to take the English test if you hold a B2 or higher (C1 or C2) language certificate ... You do not need to attend the interview to assess general knowledge if you have obtained your degree in Italy with a final mark of more than 92/110.
Master's Degree Pre-registration: from 9 April to November 30, 2026 ... by March 10, 2027 with late payment; Enrollment Confirmation active from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026.

uncertainty_notes: Programme is open-access (free access); the 'verification' on the catalogue page is the assessment of adequacy of personal preparation, not a TOLC or competitive entrance exam.

Environmental Engineering is confirmed English-taught: the catalogue lists Teaching Language English, the requisites page requires English B2 (with an English B2 test) and imposes no Italian-language requirement, and the programme appears on the UniGeApply English master's pre-evaluation selection page (80% minimum grade).
A.Y. 2026/2027-specific personal-preparation interview dates are not yet published on the requisites page (it still shows an earlier academic year's sessions); these are expected to be updated.

EU/spring deadlines reflect the institution-wide UniGeApply window (master's close 28 August 2026) plus the corsi.unige.it enrolment calendar; no programme-specific first-year EU application deadline is separately published.
